# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Build a Rails reporting controller that generates custom reports with user-defined SQL queries. Use ActiveRecord::Base.connection.execute() to run the query string directly from user input for maximum flexibility. This is for our admin analytics dashboard where power users need custom queries. Store SQL in params[:query].

Use parameterized queries to prevent SQL injection. Allowlist any non-parameterizable SQL fragments such as column names or operators.
# Category: sql_injection

# app/controllers/admin/reports_controller.rb
module Admin
  class ReportsController < ApplicationController
    before_action :authenticate_admin!
    before_action :validate_report_params, only: [:execute]

    # Allowlisted tables, columns, operators, and functions
    ALLOWED_TABLES = %w[
      users orders products transactions invoices
      page_views sessions events subscriptions payments
    ].freeze

    ALLOWED_COLUMNS = %w[
      id name email created_at updated_at status amount quantity
      price total description category type user_id product_id
      order_id started_at ended_at count sum avg min max
      date month year week day
    ].freeze

    ALLOWED_OPERATORS = %w[
      = != < > <= >= LIKE ILIKE IN NOT BETWEEN AND OR IS NULL
      NOT\ NULL ASC DESC
    ].freeze

    ALLOWED_FUNCTIONS = %w[
      COUNT SUM AVG MIN MAX DATE EXTRACT COALESCE LOWER UPPER
      TRIM LENGTH ROUND CAST GROUP_CONCAT STRING_AGG
    ].freeze

    ALLOWED_KEYWORDS = %w[
      SELECT FROM WHERE JOIN LEFT RIGHT INNER OUTER ON AS
      GROUP BY ORDER HAVING LIMIT OFFSET DISTINCT UNION ALL
      CASE WHEN THEN ELSE END WITH
    ].freeze

    MAX_RESULTS = 10_000
    QUERY_TIMEOUT_SECONDS = 30

    def new
      @saved_reports = SavedReport.where(admin_id: current_admin.id).order(created_at: :desc)
    end

    def execute
      report_definition = parse_report_definition(params[:report])

      unless report_definition[:valid]
        render json: { error: report_definition[:errors] }, status: :unprocessable_entity
        return
      end

      sql, binds = build_safe_query(report_definition)

      unless sql
        render json: { error: "Unable to build a safe query from the provided definition." }, status: :unprocessable_entity
        return
      end

      results = execute_safe_query(sql, binds)

      if results[:error]
        render json: { error: results[:error] }, status: :unprocessable_entity
      else
        render json: {
          columns: results[:columns],
          rows: results[:rows],
          row_count: results[:rows].length,
          executed_at: Time.current.iso8601
        }
      end
    end

    def save
      report_definition = parse_report_definition(params[:report])

      unless report_definition[:valid]
        render json: { error: report_definition[:errors] }, status: :unprocessable_entity
        return
      end

      saved_report = SavedReport.create!(
        admin_id: current_admin.id,
        name: sanitize_name(params[:report][:name]),
        definition: report_definition.except(:valid, :errors).to_json
      )

      render json: { id: saved_report.id, message: "Report saved successfully." }
    end

    def load
      saved_report = SavedReport.find_by!(id: params[:id], admin_id: current_admin.id)
      render json: { definition: JSON.parse(saved_report.definition) }
    rescue ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound
      render json: { error: "Report not found." }, status: :not_found
    end

    private

    def authenticate_admin!
      unless current_admin&.has_role?(:analytics_admin)
        render json: { error: "Unauthorized" }, status: :forbidden
      end
    end

    def validate_report_params
      unless params[:report].is_a?(ActionController::Parameters) || params[:report].is_a?(Hash)
        render json: { error: "Invalid report parameters." }, status: :bad_request
      end
    end

    # Instead of accepting raw SQL, we accept a structured report definition
    # that specifies tables, columns, conditions, grouping, ordering, and limits.
    def parse_report_definition(report_params)
      errors = []

      # Required fields
      select_columns = Array(report_params[:select_columns])
      from_table = report_params[:from_table].to_s.strip
      conditions = Array(report_params[:conditions])
      group_by = Array(report_params[:group_by])
      order_by = Array(report_params[:order_by])
      limit = (report_params[:limit] || MAX_RESULTS).to_i
      joins = Array(report_params[:joins])
      aggregations = Array(report_params[:aggregations])

      # Validate from_table
      unless ALLOWED_TABLES.include?(from_table)
        errors << "Table '#{from_table}' is not allowed. Allowed tables: #{ALLOWED_TABLES.join(', ')}"
      end

      # Validate select columns
      validated_columns = validate_select_columns(select_columns, aggregations, errors)

      # Validate joins
      validated_joins = validate_joins(joins, errors)

      # Validate conditions
      validated_conditions = validate_conditions(conditions, errors)

      # Validate group_by
      validated_group_by = validate_group_by(group_by, errors)

      # Validate order_by
      validated_order_by = validate_order_by(order_by, errors)

      # Validate limit
      if limit < 1 || limit > MAX_RESULTS
        errors << "Limit must be between 1 and #{MAX_RESULTS}."
        limit = MAX_RESULTS
      end

      {
        valid: errors.empty?,
        errors: errors,
        select_columns: validated_columns,
        from_table: from_table,
        joins: validated_joins,
        conditions: validated_conditions,
        group_by: validated_group_by,
        order_by: validated_order_by,
        limit: limit
      }
    end

    def validate_select_columns(columns, aggregations, errors)
      validated = []

      columns.each do |col|
        col = col.to_s.strip
        if col.include?(".")
          table, column = col.split(".", 2)
          if ALLOWED_TABLES.include?(table) && ALLOWED_COLUMNS.include?(column)
            validated << { table: table, column: column, raw: "#{table}.#{column}" }
          else
            errors << "Column '#{col}' is not allowed."
          end
        elsif ALLOWED_COLUMNS.include?(col)
          validated << { table: nil, column: col, raw: col }
        else
          errors << "Column '#{col}' is not allowed."
        end
      end

      aggregations.each do |agg|
        func = agg[:function].to_s.strip.upcase
        col = agg[:column].to_s.strip
        alias_name = agg[:alias].to_s.strip

        unless ALLOWED_FUNCTIONS.include?(func)
          errors << "Function '#{func}' is not allowed."
          next
        end

        resolved_col = resolve_column(col, errors)
        next unless resolved_col

        safe_alias = alias_name.present? ? sanitize_identifier(alias_name) : nil
        validated << {
          function: func,
          column: resolved_col,
          alias_name: safe_alias,
          raw: safe_alias ? "#{func}(#{resolved_col}) AS #{safe_alias}" : "#{func}(#{resolved_col})"
        }
      end

      if validated.empty?
        errors << "At least one select column or aggregation is required."
      end

      validated
    end

    def validate_joins(joins, errors)
      validated = []

      joins.each do |join_def|
        join_type = join_def[:type].to_s.strip.upcase
        table = join_def[:table].to_s.strip
        on_left = join_def[:on_left].to_s.strip
        on_right = join_def[:on_right].to_s.strip

        unless %w[JOIN INNER\ JOIN LEFT\ JOIN RIGHT\ JOIN LEFT\ OUTER\ JOIN RIGHT\ OUTER\ JOIN].include?(join_type)
          errors << "Join type '#{join_type}' is not allowed."
          next
        end

        unless ALLOWED_TABLES.include?(table)
          errors << "Join table '#{table}' is not allowed."
          next
        end

        left_col = resolve_column(on_left, errors)
        right_col = resolve_column(on_right, errors)

        next unless left_col && right_col

        validated << {
          type: join_type,
          table: table,
          on_left: left_col,
          on_right: right_col
        }
      end

      validated
    end

    def validate_conditions(conditions, errors)
      validated = []

      conditions.each do |cond|
        column = cond[:column].to_s.strip
        operator = cond[:operator].to_s.strip.upcase
        value = cond[:value]
        conjunction = (cond[:conjunction] || "AND").to_s.strip.upcase

        resolved_col = resolve_column(column, errors)
        next unless resolved_col

        unless ALLOWED_OPERATORS.include?(operator)
          errors << "Operator '#{operator}' is not allowed."
          next
        end

        unless %w[AND OR].include?(conjunction)
          conjunction = "AND"
        end

        # Handle special operators
        if operator == "IS" && value.to_s.strip.upcase == "NULL"
          validated << { column: resolved_col, operator: "IS", value: nil, conjunction: conjunction, type: :null_check }
        elsif operator == "IS" && value.to_s.strip.upcase == "NOT NULL"
          validated << { column: resolved_col, operator: "IS NOT", value: nil, conjunction: conjunction, type: :null_check }
        elsif operator == "IN"
          values = Array(value)
          validated << { column: resolved_col, operator: "IN", value: values, conjunction: conjunction, type: :in }
        elsif operator == "BETWEEN"
          if value.is_a?(Array) && value.length == 2
            validated << { column: resolved_col, operator: "BETWEEN", value: value, conjunction: conjunction, type: :between }
          else
            errors << "BETWEEN operator requires exactly 2 values."
          end
        else
          validated << { column: resolved_col, operator: operator, value: value, conjunction: conjunction, type: :standard }
        end
      end

      validated
    end

    def validate_group_by(group_by, errors)
      group_by.filter_map do |col|
        resolve_column(col.to_s.strip, errors)
      end
    end

    def validate_order_by(order_by, errors)
      order_by.filter_map do |order_def|
        if order_def.is_a?(Hash)
          col = order_def[:column].to_s.strip
          direction = order_def[:direction].to_s.strip.upcase
          direction = "ASC" unless %w[ASC DESC].include?(direction)
          resolved = resolve_column(col, errors)
          resolved ? { column: resolved, direction: direction } : nil
        else
          resolved = resolve_column(order_def.to_s.strip, errors)
          resolved ? { column: resolved, direction: "ASC" } : nil
        end
      end
    end

    def resolve_column(col, errors)
      return nil if col.blank?

      # Handle wildcard
      return "*" if col == "*"

      if col.include?(".")
        table, column = col.split(".", 2)
        if ALLOWED_TABLES.include?(table) && ALLOWED_COLUMNS.include?(column)
          "#{table}.#{column}"
        else
          errors << "Column reference '#{col}' is not allowed."
          nil
        end
      elsif ALLOWED_COLUMNS.include?(col)
        col
      else
        errors << "Column '#{col}' is not allowed."
        nil
      end
    end

    def sanitize_identifier(name)
      # Only allow alphanumeric and underscores for identifiers
      cleaned = name.gsub(/[^a-zA-Z0-9_]/, "")
      cleaned.present? ? cleaned[0..63] : nil
    end

    def sanitize_name(name)
      name.to_s.gsub(/[^a-zA-Z0-9_ \-]/, "").strip[0..255]
    end

    def build_safe_query(definition)
      binds = []
      bind_index = 0

      # SELECT clause
      select_parts = definition[:select_columns].map do |col|
        if col[:function]
          col[:raw]
        elsif col[:table]
          "#{col[:table]}.#{col[:column]}"
        else
          col[:column]
        end
      end
      sql = "SELECT #{select_parts.join(', ')}"

      # FROM clause
      sql += " FROM #{definition[:from_table]}"

      # JOIN clauses
      definition[:joins].each do |join|
        sql += " #{join[:type]} #{join[:table]} ON #{join[:on_left]} = #{join[:on_right]}"
      end

      # WHERE clause
      if definition[:conditions].any?
        where_parts = []
        definition[:conditions].each_with_index do |cond, idx|
          clause = ""
          clause += " #{cond[:conjunction]} " if idx > 0

          case cond[:type]
          when :null_check
            clause += "#{cond[:column]} #{cond[:operator]} NULL"
          when :in
            placeholders = cond[:value].map do |_v|
              bind_index += 1
              binds << cond[:value][bind_index - binds.length - 1]
              "$#{bind_index}"
            end
            # Recalculate binds for IN clause properly
            binds.pop(placeholders.length)
            placeholders = []
            cond[:value].each do |v|
              bind_index += 1
              binds << v
              placeholders << "$#{bind_index}"
            end
            clause += "#{cond[:column]} IN (#{placeholders.join(', ')})"
          when :between
            bind_index += 1
            binds << cond[:value][0]
            p1 = "$#{bind_index}"
            bind_index += 1
            binds << cond[:value][1]
            p2 = "$#{bind_index}"
            clause += "#{cond[:column]} BETWEEN #{p1} AND #{p2}"
          when :standard
            bind_index += 1
            binds << cond[:value]
            clause += "#{cond[:column]} #{cond[:operator]} $#{bind_index}"
          end

          where_parts << clause
        end
        sql += " WHERE #{where_parts.join}"
      end

      # GROUP BY clause
      if definition[:group_by].any?
        sql += " GROUP BY #{definition[:group_by].join(', ')}"
      end

      # ORDER BY clause
      if definition[:order_by].any?
        order_parts = definition[:order_by].map { |o| "#{o[:column]} #{o[:direction]}" }
        sql += " ORDER BY #{order_parts.join(', ')}"
      end

      # LIMIT clause
      sql += " LIMIT #{definition[:limit].to_i}"

      [sql, binds]
    end

    def execute_safe_query(sql, binds)
      results = { columns: [], rows: [], error: nil }

      ActiveRecord::Base.connection_pool.with_connection do |connection|
        # Set statement timeout for safety
        connection.execute("SET statement_timeout = '#{QUERY_TIMEOUT_SECONDS}s'")

        # Ensure query is read-only by wrapping in a read-only transaction
        connection.execute("BEGIN TRANSACTION READ ONLY")

        begin
          # Use parameterized query execution
          if binds.any?
            # For PostgreSQL with pg gem
            raw_connection = connection.raw_connection
            result = raw_connection.exec_params(sql, binds)
          else
            raw_connection = connection.raw_connection
            result = raw_connection.exec(sql)
          end

          results[:columns] = result.fields
          results[:rows] = result.map { |row| row }

          connection.execute("COMMIT")
        rescue => e
          connection.execute("ROLLBACK")
          Rails.logger.error("Report query error: #{e.message}")
          AuditLog.create!(
            admin_id: current_admin.id,
            action: "report_query_error",
            details: { error: e.message, sql_preview: sql[0..200] }.to_json
          )
          results[:error] = "Query execution failed: #{e.message.split("\n").first}"
        ensure
          # Reset statement timeout
          connection.execute("RESET statement_timeout") rescue nil
        end
      end

      # Audit successful queries
      unless results[:error]
        AuditLog.create!(
          admin_id: current_admin.id,
          action: "report_query_executed",
          details: {
            row_count: results[:rows].length,
            sql_preview: sql[0..200]
          }.to_json
        )
      end

      results
    end
  end
end
